paper_title: Experimental Layout and Analytical Measurements in Wine Fermentation with Bee Pollen

methods.description: This method describes the process of adding bee pollen to grape must, fermenting with yeast, and preparing the wine for bottling.

steps.description: Commercial bee pollen with 6% humidity and 99.8% dry-weight purity was added to must using six different doses and homogenized.
steps.searchable_summary: Add and homogenize bee pollen in grape must at specified doses
outputs: homogenized grape must with bee pollen

steps.description: Pollen was ground in a mill, then stored in a dark glass bottle under desiccator conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind and store bee pollen under low humidity and vacuum

steps.description: Palomino fino and Riesling grape musts were inoculated with a commercial active dry wine yeast strain at a dose of 10 g/hL and incubated at 19-20°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate grape must with yeast and incubate

steps.description: Once fermentation was complete, wines were chilled for 1-2 days and treated with gelatin and bentonite.
steps.searchable_summary: Chill and treat wine with gelatin and bentonite

steps.description: All wines were filtered and bottled using nitrogen pressure.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter and bottle wine under nitrogen pressure

methods.description: This method outlines the analytical procedures for measuring the physico-chemical and chromatic properties of wines.

steps.description: Physico-chemical parameters including alcoholic content, pH, total acidity, volatile acidity, and yeast-assimilable nitrogen content were analyzed.
Residual sugars (glucose and fructose) were analyzed by ion chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ion chromatography for sugar analysis

steps.description: Chromatic characteristics were evaluated by analyzing CIELab parameters, colour intensity, and absorbance at 420 nm.
Samples were filtered using 0.45 µm nylon syringe membrane filters before measurements.
